REQUEST_CHANGES
The docs add LM Studio performance/model-management endpoints and the unknown fallback, but they still do not document freshness rules and do not clearly map any context-window/context-capacity signal to a concrete endpoint.

rationale: verification_command: rg -q "api/v1/chat" docs/research/provider-signal-matrix-2026-05-06.md && rg -q "api/v1/models" docs/research/provider-signal-matrix-2026-05-06.md && rg -q "No verified native cache counter is exposed" docs/research/provider-signal-matrix-2026-05-06.md && rg -q "Freshness Rules" docs/research/provider-signal-matrix-2026-05-06.md && rg -q "Follow-up bead: implement an LM Studio utilization probe" docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-providers.md
status: complete
reason: The bead is already satisfied by existing documentation in the worktree. The provider-signal matrix note maps LM Studio to concrete endpoints and signal classes: native `POST /api/v1/chat` stats for performance, `GET /api/v0/models/<id>` for context length, `GET /api/v1/models` plus model load/unload/download endpoints for model management, and `lms server status --json` as a non-utilization CLI status surface. The note also records that cache pressure remains unknown, that freshness is per-successful-response/context lookup with unknown on first failure, and that the live LM Studio utilization probe remains a documented follow-up gap. FEAT-003 carries the same gap language and next-step bead text.
